actual.name: actual
actual.summary: A system that receives a person's half-formed concept, unfinished inspirations, and visualized vision — then wanders across domains (math, physics, literature, theater, anything) to find both literal and metaphorical prior work that can either inherit from, extend, or give structural skeleton to that concept. The system is anchored by three data points: current pursuit, undone threads, and the vision the user holds in their mind. It draws on chaos theory (unpredictable traversal), Markov chains (state-transition wandering), and Heisenberg-style partial knowledge (skeleton without full resolution).

node_records.embedding_text: a vague, unarticulated idea or inspiration given as the starting seed for a discovery process

node_records.embedding_text: a minimal structured input composed of current pursuit, unfinished inspirations, and visualized end-state that together give a system enough signal to orient itself
autonomous explorers ranging across unrelated knowledge domains to surface structural matches to a seed problem

search_queries: standing on shoulders of giants research
node_records.embedding_text: building forward from an existing published concept or research result rather than starting from zero
a search process whose next step is structurally unpredictable, modeled on chaotic systems where trajectory is trackable in retrospect but not forecastable

node_records.embedding_text: each step in exploration depends only on the current state, not the full history, producing a memoryless but locally coherent path through knowledge space

search_queries: uncertainty principle applied to knowledge systems
node_records.embedding_text: a system that operates on incomplete structural knowledge, providing orientation and shape without resolving the concept to full clarity

essence.name: essence
essence.summary: The core tension here is between incompleteness and forward motion — the user's concept is not yet fully formed, yet the system must act on it productively. The system must hold a vague signal stable enough to anchor wandering, while allowing that wandering to be genuinely unpredictable. This requires a paradox: the anchor must be strong enough to score relevance, but loose enough not to collapse the search into obvious territory. The productive energy lives in the gap between the known skeleton and the unknown full form — and the system's value is precisely that it does not resolve that gap prematurely, but finds resonant structures that let the user resolve it themselves.

node_records.embedding_text: a state in which partial articulation is not a deficiency but the generative condition that makes discovery possible
a system that is simultaneously unconstrained in its traversal and oriented by a fixed structural reference point it cannot abandon
finding meaningful correspondence between two things that share no surface vocabulary but exhibit the same underlying dynamic or tension
maintaining an imprecise input as a fixed reference without forcing it into premature precision, so that it can attract relevant matches without collapsing the search space
a path that appears random in prospect but reveals meaningful structure only when traced backward — the logic is visible in the wake, not the heading
operating on structural shape alone — the bones of an idea — without needing the flesh of full articulation before action begins

search_queries: standing on prior art while transcending it
node_records.embedding_text: the tension between faithfully building on prior work and departing from it far enough to constitute genuine advancement rather than repetition

mechanism.name: mechanism
nodes: partial knowledge as operating condition, not deficiency
memoryless local coherence preserves global unpredictability
structural match scores across surface distance
three-point anchor as minimum viable orientation
concept advancement requires structural inheritance plus departure vector
mechanism.summary: The causal primitive operating here is: a system whose output value is proportional to its traversal unpredictability cannot be driven by deterministic search, yet must not be driven by pure noise — it requires a structural attractor that scores resonance without specifying path. The three-anchor brief functions as this attractor: it is sparse enough to leave the search space open, dense enough to reject irrelevant matches, and structurally rich enough to score partial matches across unrelated domains. The Heisenberg move — accepting partial knowledge as the operating condition rather than a deficiency to correct — is what allows the system to act before the concept is fully formed. The Markov move — caring only about the current state, not the full history — is what keeps traversal locally coherent without globally predictable. The chaos move — accepting that the trajectory is only knowable in retrospect — is what preserves the spontaneity that makes the output valuable. Together: sparse structural anchor + memoryless local coherence + retrospective-only path legibility = a system that finds what deterministic search cannot, because it does not know where it is going.

node_records.embedding_text: a system whose goal is unpredictable discovery must be anchored by a signal rich enough to score relevance but sparse enough not to collapse the search into predictable territory

node_records.embedding_text: in systems where value comes from unexpected discovery, the path can only be understood after it is completed — forward prediction of the path destroys the value

node_records.text: structural match scores across surface distance
node_records.embedding_text: relevance in cross-domain discovery is determined by shared structural dynamics, not shared vocabulary — a scoring mechanism must operate at the level of tension and constraint, not topic

node_records.embedding_text: to move a concept forward rather than merely repeat it, a system must identify what the prior work structurally established and where its unresolved tensions point — the departure vector lives in the gap

problem.name: problem
problem.content: I'm currently working on a system that helps people advance their concepts or the work they're currently working on. If there's a case or research that's already published, based on that inheritance, based on that work, we can advance it. If I'm the one who didn't want to be at the same level and I'm just having some similar inspirations and I wanted to take that method or research paper or concept to a next level — how can I do this? I'm making the system like that. Or I'm having a whole new concept: if there's a system I'm building and I wanted to make a feature out of it and I'm having some vague inspirations of that system but I don't know how to articulate it, I don't know how to make it live — there's a system that can do that part of it.

vision.name: vision
vision.content: If a person tells the inspiration, the pursuit, and any unfinished work or unfinished threads in their mind — some of the big inspirations — to the model, then the model has something to anchor against. These three information points — the current pursuit (whether half-baked or not), the undone inspirations, and the vision the user is actually visualizing in their brain — can give enough data to the model to keep intact its score and keep wandering around through the Internet to find native and metaphorical work in the respective domains, whether it's math, physics, psychology, or even theater, literature, anything.

current_map.name: current_map
current_map.content: Based on what I told you about this system, you can't be working on it without some theoretical seeds. I stumbled into many concepts — Heisenberg, Markov chain, chaos theory. Chaos theory is what lets the model wander the Internet unhindered, like total chaos. There's no prediction of where the model will go next, but we can track it. But that track is the past, not the future. We cannot predict where it will end up. That is the spontaneousness I want to give to the model. The Markov chain also plays an important role in my thinking. The same goes with the Heisenberg principle — it doesn't provide a clear understanding, but a half-understanding. Just a structure. Just a skeleton.
